articles.title: Laid-off Oracle workers tried to negotiate better severance. Oracle said no.
articles.description: Some found out they didn't qualify for WARN Act protections like two-months notice because the company had classified them as remote workers.

articles.title: San Francisco’s housing market has lost its mind
articles.description: The invisible force behind all of this is no mystery to anyone paying attention to the city's tech economy. San Francisco is home to some of the most valuable private companies in the world, and their employees have been quietly accumulating — and, increasingly, cashing out — fortunes.

articles.title: Prime Video follows Netflix and Disney by adding a TikTok-like ‘Clips’ feed in its app
articles.description: The Clips feed aims to enable discovery by offering users a scrollable feed with short snippets of shows and movies.

articles.title: Intel’s comeback story is even wilder than it seems
articles.description: Intel's stock has risen a stunning 490% over the past year, a bet by Wall Street that may be running well ahead of the company's actual turnaround.

articles.title: Cloudflare says AI made 1,100 jobs obsolete, even as revenue hit a record high
articles.description: Cloudflare announced its first large-scale layoff. CEO Matthew Prince says because of AI efficiency gains, the company doesn't need as many support roles.

articles.title: Porsche shutters e-bike, battery, software subsidiaries as part of company overhaul
articles.description: More than 500 people will be affected by the closures. “We must refocus on our core business,” Porsche CEO and executive chair Michael Leiters said in a statement. "This is the indispensable foundation for a successful strategic realignment. This forces us to make painful cuts — including our subsidiaries."

articles.title: Mother Ventures is looking at moms as the ‘economic engine’
articles.description: The VC firm, which focuses on mothers as consumers, raised a $10 million debut fund.

articles.title: Uber partner Avride is under investigation for self-driving crashes
articles.description: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration has opened an investigation into Avride after identifying more than a dozen crashes and one minor injury.

articles.title: Poland says hackers breached water treatment plants, and the US is facing the same threat
articles.description: A report by Poland’s top intelligence agency accused Russia of sabotage and hacking activities against the country’s military and civilian infrastructure.

articles.title: US defense contractor who sold hacking tools to Russian broker ordered to pay $10M to former employers
articles.description: Former cybersecurity executive Peter Williams stole several surveillance and hacking tools and sold them for $1.3 million to a Russian broker that works with Putin’s government.

articles.title: Pentagon releases UFO files on new website
articles.description: The initial set of files housed on the site will include those containing unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) videos, photos, and original source documents from across the entire U.S. government. The materials have been reviewed for security purposes, but many have "not yet been analyzed for resolution of any anomalies," the Department's statement read.

articles.title: Truecaller slashes 70 jobs amid declining ad sales
articles.description: Truecaller's ad revenue declined by 44%, leading to the layoffs.

articles.title: Live only at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026: Why most founders are already behind on raising a Series A in 2027
articles.description: Learn what it takes to build a Series A in 2027 from top VCs at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026, taking place at San Francisco’s Moscone West on October 13-15. Register today to hear this session live on the Builders Stage.

articles.title: Last 24 hours to get 50% off a second pass to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
articles.description: Last day to buy one pass and get a second one at 50% off to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Bring a partner, co-founder, or colleague at half off. Register now.

articles.title: Lime, the Uber-backed micromobility company, files for IPO
articles.description: After years of hints and preparation, Lime plans to enter the public markets. The company intends to list on ​Nasdaq under the ticker symbol "LIME." Lime did not share terms of the offering.

articles.title: The biggest US power grid is under strain from AI — and no one is happy
articles.description: PJM Interconnection — which oversees the grid for some of the densest data center developments on Earth — wants to overhaul itself. Not everyone thinks it's up to the task.

articles.title: Airbnb says AI now writes 60% of its new code
articles.description: Airbnb also said its customer support AI bot now handles 40% of issues without escalating to a human agent.

articles.title: The fax machine is the bottleneck in US healthcare, and VCs are starting to notice
articles.description: Like many AI companies automating work that humans currently do, Basata will eventually face a harder question about where the line is between augmenting workers and displacing them. For now, the founders say the administrative staff they work with aren't worried about that; they're more worried about drowning.

articles.title: Ramp in talks to hit $40B+ valuation, 6 months after reaching $32B
articles.description: The company is reportedly in talks to raise another $750 million at a pre-money valuation of more than $40 billion. It last raised in November at $32 billion.

articles.title: OpenAI launches new voice intelligence features in its API
articles.description: The new features could be handy for customer service systems, but OpenAI says they have applications that work across a variety of other fields, including education and creator platforms.

articles.title: Trump reportedly plans to fire FDA Commissioner Marty Makary
articles.description: The plan isn't final and could change, but his ouster would be no surprise.

articles.title: ABC refuses to capitulate to Trump admin, fights FCC probe into The View
articles.description: FCC chair hasn't been able to bully ABC and owner Disney into submission.

articles.title: Sony says "efficient" AI tools will lead to even more games flooding the market
articles.description: But human artists still "must remain at the center," PlayStation maker says.

articles.title: The unprecedented and deadly cruise ship hantavirus outbreak, explained
articles.description: "This is not COVID," and other reasons why risk to the public is currently low.

articles.title: Course correction: Google to link more sources in AI Overviews
articles.description: Google's AI search will start citing its sources in several new ways.

articles.title: Court rules Trump's 10% tariff is just as illegal as the tariff it replaced
articles.description: Trump's vow to impose tariffs a "different way" already has the tech industry on edge.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.description: Across the country, schools and colleges postpone year-end tests.

articles.title: Elon Musk faces criminal probe in France after ignoring summons in X case
articles.description: France threatens criminal charges if Musk doesn't appear for questioning.

articles.title: Chrome's 4GB AI model isn't new, but you're not wrong for being confused
articles.description: You can stop Chrome from taking up 4GB of storage for local AI, but that shouldn't be your problem.

articles.title: Engineers at NASA's Jet Propulsion Lab make a breakthrough in rotor technology
articles.description: Testing shows rotor blades won't disintegrate when they spin at supersonic speed.

articles.title: DNA identifies four more crew members of doomed Franklin expedition
articles.description: Three served on the HMS Erebus ; the fourth was Petty Officer Harry Peglar of the HMS Terror .

articles.title: Which Macs are suffering from shortages—and where are things getting worse?
articles.description: There are a couple signs of strain beyond the MacBook Neo and the desktops.

articles.title: How climate change makes your allergies worse
articles.description: As pollen season worsens, allergies compound with other climate health hazards.

articles.title: The Nintendo Switch 2 is getting more expensive later this year
articles.description: "Changes in market conditions" lead to $50 price bump on Sept. 1.

articles.title: The US military just released a bunch of UAP files, but there's no there there
articles.description: Here at Ars Technica, we do not preclude the possibility that aliens have visited Earth.

articles.title: Everyone’s a loser in Strait of Hormuz game that simulates global crisis
articles.description: The game asks players to find the least worst options for a shipping chokepoint.

articles.title: Rocket Report: Alpha Block 2 coming this summer; Falcon sets booster landing mark
articles.description: "The deciding factor was what we felt like was the team’s impact to humanity."

articles.title: DHS can’t create vast DNA database to track ICE critics, lawsuit says
articles.description: Lawsuit accuses DHS of plugging DNA database into ICE surveillance machine.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.description: The developer of Firefox says it has "completely bought in" on AI-assisted bug discovery.

articles.title: Google unveils screenless Fitbit Air and Google Health app to replace Fitbit
articles.description: The $100 Fitbit Air is available for preorder today.

tech.summary:
今日科技动态摘要

1. **科技公司裁员与业务调整持续**：甲骨文拒绝被裁员工提高遣散费的谈判请求；Truecaller因广告销售下滑裁员70人；保时捷关闭电动自行车、电池及软件子公司以进行业务重组；Cloudflare承认AI导致1100个岗位消失，尽管营收创历史新高。

2. **流媒体与内容平台功能趋同**：亚马逊Prime Video效仿Netflix和迪士尼，在应用中新增类似TikTok的短视频“Clips”功能，推动平台社交化与短视频化趋势。

3. **自动驾驶与网络安全风险加剧**：Uber合作方Avride因自动驾驶事故被调查；波兰称黑客入侵水处理设施，美国面临同样威胁；美国国防承包商因向俄罗斯中介出售黑客工具被判赔偿前雇主1000万美元。

4. **IPO与资本市场动态**：Uber支持的共享出行公司Lime提交IPO申请；TechCrunch提醒创始人2027年A轮融资竞争已提前加剧。

5. **住房与新兴投资趋势**：旧金山房地产市场出现非理性波动；风投公司Mother Ventures将母亲群体视为“经济引擎”，关注家庭消费与女性经济。

今日最值得关注的发展是：AI技术虽推动企业营收增长，但已开始显著取代传统岗位，同时科技行业在裁员、业务重组与新兴投资方向上的调整正加速展开。

ai.summary:
今日AI动态摘要

1. **网络安全与专业模型**：CyberSecQwen-4B强调防御性网络需要小型、专用、可本地运行的模型；OpenAI推出GPT-5.5及GPT-5.5-Cyber，以扩展网络领域的可信访问。
2. **模型架构与训练优化**：EMO提出通过预训练混合专家实现涌现模块性；vLLM从V0到V1在强化学习中强调“先正确再修正”的原则。
3. **AI应用与创意产业**：Google展示创意人士用AI为小企业制作广告；Google与XPRIZE等合作推出350万美元“未来视觉”电影竞赛；OpenAI测试ChatGPT广告功能，并引入“可信联系人”功能。
4. **语音与API能力升级**：OpenAI推出新API模型以增强语音智能；Gemini API通过Webhooks减少长任务延迟。
5. **行业基准与工具改进**：Hugging Face在开放ASR排行榜中加入“Benchmaxxer Repellant”防作弊机制；Google搜索推出5项AI辅助园艺技巧。

**今日最值得关注的发展**：OpenAI与Google在网络安全、创意产业和语音智能领域同步推出多项关键产品与竞赛，标志着AI正从通用能力向行业专属、可信与创意应用深度拓展。

articles.title: Tokyo Trial debunks notorious Yasukuni Shrine's inverted narrative
DC gala shooting suspect voices grievances against US administration in writings to family: media reports

cctv.summary:
今日新闻联播动态摘要

1. 东京审判揭露靖国神社歪曲历史叙事，强调国际社会对二战罪行的正义审判不可篡改。
2. 美国华盛顿特区游行枪击案嫌疑人在写给家人的信件中表达对美国政府的不满，引发对国内社会矛盾的关注。

总结：今日最值得关注的发展是东京审判相关报道有力驳斥了靖国神社的历史修正主义，同时美国国内枪击事件暴露出的社会裂痕亦需警惕。
